priority_basis: No active frontier_research item remained in the canonical queue. R-023 explicitly left persistent storage, concurrency, reload behavior, crash recovery, and retention policy unresolved.
finding: An in-memory replay gate does not provide one-time acceptance across reloads or concurrent consumers. The acceptance identity must be consumed through a persistent exclusive-lock transaction with bounded retention and fail-closed corruption behavior.
verification: 6 deterministic Node tests passed locally, including an eight-way concurrent duplicate race in which exactly one consumer succeeded.

claim_states.observed: The prior gate accepted a caller-provided in-memory seen-ID array; the new persistent ledger passed six tests.
claim_states.inferred: Exclusive-create locking plus replacement rename prevents duplicate acceptance among cooperating processes on one shared filesystem.
claim_states.proposed: Place the ledger immediately after R-023 validation and retain only opaque IDs and acceptance times.
claim_states.superseded: Treating an in-memory array as adequate replay protection across navigation, reload, or competing runtime instances.
claim_states.unresolved: Stale-lock recovery, directory fsync durability, multi-host coordination, browser storage adaptation, bfcache integration, and production retention policy remain unresolved.

handoff: Vercel Studio should integrate the R-023 gate with this ledger, then add stale-lock recovery and browser-appropriate atomic storage. Test tab races, bfcache restoration, abrupt termination, and retention-window replay.
